title: fixes for tests
body:
- set requred version of pillow to 9.5.0
- test-ffmpeg-reader - change duration number to match video
- ffmpeg-reader - add displaymatrix to be read as video rotation
- config set binary to autodetect

<!--
Please tick when you have done these. They don't need to all be completed before the PR is submitted.
Delete them if they are not appropriate for this pull request.
-->
- [x] I have provided code that clearly demonstrates the bug and that only works correctly when applying this fix
- [x] I have added suitable tests demonstrating a fixed bug or new/changed feature to the test suite in `tests/`
addresses issue #2183

step_plan.summary: Fixes test failures by aligning dependencies and FFmpeg-related parsing with real-world outputs: pin Pillow, correct duration expectations, add rotation detection via displaymatrix side-data, and default FFmpeg binary selection to autodetection.

steps.operation: Extend FFmpeg stderr parsing to detect rotation from displaymatrix side data and populate infos['video_rotation'] (falling back to existing 'rotate' metadata when present)
steps.reason: Some FFmpeg builds report rotation via stream side-data (e.g., “displaymatrix: rotation of … degrees”) instead of stream metadata “rotate”. Tests and runtime behavior need consistent rotation detection regardless of which representation FFmpeg emits.
steps.side_effects: May change detected rotation for some files/FFmpeg versions; downstream width/height swap logic and any code that relies on infos['video_rotation'] will start working for displaymatrix-only cases; rotation-related tests may need updated/expanded expectations.

steps.operation: Update metadata casting/normalization so rotation values extracted from either 'rotate' or displaymatrix parsing are consistently numeric (degrees) and comparable
steps.reason: The parser currently type-casts only certain fields (notably 'rotate'); with displaymatrix-derived rotation introduced, the code should ensure the final stored rotation is a numeric degree value (and normalized if needed) so other components can safely use it.
steps.side_effects: Could surface previously silent parsing issues (e.g., unexpected string formats) and may require minor test adjustments if rotation rounding/normalization changes.

steps.operation: Adjust duration parsing/selection logic used by ffmpeg_parse_infos so the returned duration matches the actual fixture video duration used in decode mode, improving test stability across FFmpeg outputs
steps.reason: The test suite indicates a mismatch between hardcoded expected durations and what FFmpeg reports/decodes for the fixture; duration extraction should be robust (e.g., prefer decoded 'time=' when decode_file=True, otherwise container 'Duration:') to avoid fragile expectations.
steps.side_effects: May change reported duration in some scenarios (especially VFR/stream-copy edge cases); could impact any user code that depends on the exact parsing behavior, and may require updating related tests.

steps.operation: Ensure video reader initialization correctly applies rotation discovered via displaymatrix (including proper width/height swap for 90/270 and consistent angle conventions)
steps.reason: Once new rotation sources are parsed, the reader must apply them consistently; any differences in sign or normalization between 'rotate' and displaymatrix-derived angles must not break dimension correction and frame orientation logic.
steps.side_effects: Could alter the perceived size/orientation of rotated videos; might require updating golden expectations in rotation-related tests and could affect any downstream code that assumes pre-rotation dimensions.

steps.operation: Update ffmpeg reader duration test expectations to match the fixture’s real decoded duration and the adjusted duration parsing behavior
steps.reason: The PR notes the expected duration value in the test is incorrect for the current fixture video; tests should assert the correct duration returned by ffmpeg_parse_infos for decode and non-decode paths.
steps.side_effects: Tests become less brittle but will lock in the new expected value; if fixture video changes again, this test will need recalibration.

steps.operation: Extend/adjust rotation parsing test to cover displaymatrix-derived rotation (and keep compatibility with existing 'rotate' metadata parsing)
steps.reason: To prevent regressions, the test suite should explicitly validate that displaymatrix rotation is recognized and mapped into infos['video_rotation'] correctly, since this is a key new behavior in the PR.
steps.side_effects: May require adding a new test asset or simulating ffmpeg stderr output; if FFmpeg output varies across versions, assertions may need to be tolerant (e.g., normalized degrees).

steps.operation: Update end-to-end rotation correctness test to pass under the new rotation detection source (displaymatrix) and any duration/rotation normalization changes
steps.reason: Rotation correctness should be validated through the full read/write/re-read pipeline; changes in rotation detection can alter this behavior, so this test must reflect the updated rotation parsing and application rules.
steps.side_effects: May require updating expected dimensions/orientation checks; could expose differences across FFmpeg builds, potentially requiring conditional logic or looser assertions if variability is unavoidable.
